zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville於大約1116年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾利爾布恩時，他的母親Millicent Brabant18歲，父親Richard Camville28歲。他於大約1135年與Melisende de Rethel在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在塞浦路斯被列為governor of cyprus 。他去世於1191年在巴勒斯坦享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville于大约1116年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾利爾布恩时，他的母亲Millicent Brabant18岁，父亲Richard Camville28岁。他于大约1135年与Melisende de Rethel在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他在塞浦路斯被列为governor of cyprus 。他去世于1191年在巴勒斯坦享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Camville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French; English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the places in Calvados and Seine-Maritime named Carville, from the Scandinavian personal name Kári + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Cearbhaill or Mac Cearbhaill, see Carroll .

variants.name: Carroll
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Cearbhaill or Mac Cearbhaill ‘descendant (or son) of Cearbhall’, a personal name perhaps based on cearbh ‘hacking’ and hence originally a byname for a butcher or a fierce warrior.
English and Scottish: variant of   Carrell  .

variants.name: Garfield
meanings: English (Midlands): topographic name from Middle English gor(e), gar(e) ‘triangular piece’ + feld ‘open country, field’ (Old English gār + feld), for someone who lived by a triangular piece of land.
English: alternatively, sometimes perhaps a variant of Carville (a name of Norman origin), with excrescent final -d.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Lilbourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Milburn
meanings: English (Northumberland, Cumberland and Durham): habitational name from any of the places in Westmorland, Northumberland, Somerset, Dorset or Wiltshire, all derived from Old English myln ‘mill’ + burna ‘spring, stream’. There has been some confusion with Melbourne .

nameMeanings.name: Stanton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

variants.name: Staunton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: Stainton
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and northern England): habitational name from any of various places called Stainton, including Stainton by Langworth, Market Stainton, and Stainton le Vale (all Lincolnshire), Stainton Dale (North Yorkshire), and Stainton (Cumberland, Durham, Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Westmorland, Yorkshire, and North Yorkshire). The placenames derive either from Old Norse steinn ‘stone, rock’ or from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’, later replaced by Old Norse steinn, + Old English tūn. The name was probably sometimes confused with or absorbed by Stanton .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville was born about 1116, in Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, England, his mother, Millicent Brabant, was 18 and his father, Richard Camville, was 28. He married Melisende de Rethel about 1135, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as governor of cyprus  in Cyprus. He died in 1191, in Palestine, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville est né environ 1116, à Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, sa mère, Millicent Brabant, avait 18 ans et son père, Richard Camville, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Melisende de Rethel environ 1135, à Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est governor of cyprus  à Chypre. Il est décédé en 1191, à Palestine, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville um 1116 in Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war seine Mutter Millicent Brabant 18 und sein Vater Richard Camville 28. Er hat um 1135 in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England Melisende de Rethel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als governor of cyprus  in Zypern aufgeführt. Er starb 1191 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Palästina.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville è nato il circa 1116 nel Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, England, il suo madre, Millicent Brabant, aveva 18 anni e il suo padre, Richard Camville, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Melisende de Rethel circa 1135, nel Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come governor of cyprus  nel Cipro. Morì nel 1191, nel Palestina, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camvilleが約1116年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアリルボウルンで生まれた時，母Millicent Brabantは18歳，父Richard Camvilleは28歳であった。約1135年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトでMelisende de Rethelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。キプロスでの職業はgovernor of cyprus と記録されている。彼は1191年にパレスチナで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville(이)가 대략 1116년 영국 노르섬프토실르 릴보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 어머니 Millicent Brabant(은)는 18세였고, 그의 아버지 Richard Camville(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1135년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 Melisende de Rethel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 키프로스에서 governor of cyprus 이었다. 그는 1191년 팔레스타인에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville nasceu aproximadamente 1116, em Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, sua mãe, Millicent Brabant, tinha 18 anos e seu pai, Richard Camville, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Melisende de Rethel aproximadamente 1135, em Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como governor of cyprus  em Chipre. Ele faleceu em 1191, em Palestina, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville родился около 1116 г. в местности лилбурн, нортамптоншир, Англия, его матери по имени Millicent Brabant было 18 лет, а отцу, которого звали Richard Camville, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Melisende de Rethel около 1135 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии governor of cyprus  в местности Кипр. Он умер в 1191 г. в местности Палестина в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Lilbourne Stanton De Camville nació aproximadamente 1116, en Lilbourne, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su mamá, Millicent Brabant, tenía 18 años y su papá, Richard Camville, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Melisende de Rethel aproximadamente 1135, en Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era governor of cyprus  en Chipre. Murió en 1191, en Palestina, a la edad de 76 años.
